Local News

Got a news story? email us: newsdesk@beyondradio.co.uk

  • PHOTOS: Police close road after hole appears on Morecambe industrial estate

    Police have closed off a road through an industrial estate in Morecambe after a hole appeared.

  • BREAKING NEWS: Morecambe FC sale given green light by English Football League

    The long-awaited sale of Morecambe Football Club has been cleared by the English Football League.

  • Archbishop of York visit ON but concert OFF at Lancaster Priory due to King's visit

    Events at Lancaster Priory this weekend have been affected by the King's visit but a service attended by the Archbishop of York will still go ahead.

  • How to apply to be a Freeman of the city of Lancaster

    Applications are being invited for one of Lancaster’s most historic traditions - becoming a Freeman or Freewoman of the city.

  • DJ to headline dance music 35th birthday party in Morecambe after surviving horror car crash

    One of dance music's best known DJs will headline a 35th anniversary night in Morecambe after surviving a horrific car crash.

  • Former head teacher to leave role as CEO of Lancaster youth charity

    A former Morecambe head teacher is moving on from her role as CEO of a Lancaster charity for young people.

  • Red Arrows due to fly over Lancaster and Morecambe District

    Fans of the Red Arrows are hoping to catch a glimpse of them in a scheduled flight over the Lancaster and Morecambe District.

  • Heartbroken family pay tribute to man who died after M6 collision near Lancaster

    A family have paid a loving tribute to a man with ‘a heart of a lion’ who died following a collision on the M6 near Lancaster.

Local Sport News

Got a sports story? email us: newsdesk@beyondradio.co.uk

  • BREAKING NEWS: Morecambe FC sale given green light by English Football League

    The long-awaited sale of Morecambe Football Club has been cleared by the English Football League.

  • Morecambe motorbike legend John McGuinness finishes 7th and 16th at TTs

    John McGuinness MBE finished in seventh and 16th place in his two starts at this year's Isle of Man TT races.

  • London Marathon and Wray Scarecrow results for Lancaster and Morecambe runners

    Here are the latest senior race results for Lancaster and Morecambe Athletics Club including the London Marathon and an annual scarecrow festival 10k near Lancaster.

  • Lancaster boxer Niall Fielding stays undefeated with fifth pro win

    Lancaster boxer Niall Fielding remained undefeated with a points win in Manchester.

South Cumbria News

Got a news story? email us: newsdesk@beyondradio.co.uk

  • Restored shelter improves passenger experience at Silverdale station

    The waiting shelter at Silverdale station has been restored, improving the experience for customers travelling along the scenic Cumbrian coast.

  • Boys, 10, arrested on suspicion of burglary in Kirkby Lonsdale

    Three 10-year-old boys have been arrested on suspicion of burglary in Kirkby Lonsdale.

  • 'Game-changing' new trains and extra services arrive on West Coast Main Line

    New revamped trains and extra services have been added to the West Coast Main Line as a nationwide shake-up of timetables came into effect.

  • Crews tackle fire started on Morecambe Bay coastal cliff edge

    Emergency services say fire crews had to put themselves in unnecessary danger to tackle a fire on a Morecambe Bay cliff edge over the weekend.

What's On

Got an event? email us: whatson@beyondradio.co.uk

  • What's On Guide WC June 9

    Here is our What's On Guide for the coming week of entertainment.

  • DJ to headline dance music 35th birthday party in Morecambe after surviving horror car crash

    One of dance music's best known DJs will headline a 35th anniversary night in Morecambe after surviving a horrific car crash.

  • Goth and post-punk 'Otherworlds' festival to make debut at Morecambe Winter Gardens

    A festival bringing together 1980s goth and punk bands - held to celebrate the Summer Solstice - will be held at the Winter Gardens in Morecambe for the first time.

  • INTERVIEW: New Lancaster festival getting back to city's Wild Roots

    A celebration of the natural world and a chance to explore the beautiful green spaces of Lancaster is coming to the city.

Station News

  • IN SESSION: The Size

    We were delighted to welcome local favourites, The Size, into the studio for a session in the Beyond Radio Live Lounge this week.

  • INTERVIEWS: Beyond Radio hosts day of live music at Lancaster community festival

    Beyond Radio has hosted the live music stage at a popular community festival in Lancaster.

  • Beyond Radio welcomes Andy Lawson as new Breakfast show host

    Beyond Radio is delighted to welcome Andy Lawson as the new host of our Breakfast show.

  • Thank you to Beyond Radio presenter Phill Hayward as he moves from Daytime show

    Beyond Radio would like to thank our mid-morning host Phill Hayward, who is stepping down from his show as part of our revamped daytime schedule.

Business News

  • INTERVIEW: "A dream come true" as new craft ale bar opens on Morecambe Promenade

    A new craft ale bar has opened inside an iconic former restaurant on the Prom to a hugely positive response from locals and visitors alike.

  • New McDonalds restaurant coming to Morecambe

    A new McDonalds is set to open in a closed-down former restaurant in Morecambe after plans were approved.

  • Turkish takeaway opens in Morecambe

    A new takeaway serving traditional Turkish cuisine has opened in Morecambe.

Local Music News

Got a local music story? email us: newsdesk@beyondradio.co.uk

  • IN SESSION: The Size

    We were delighted to welcome local favourites, The Size, into the studio for a session in the Beyond Radio Live Lounge this week.

  • INTERVIEW: Rock band Massive Wagons to play two gigs at Lancaster University

    Top rock band Massive Wagons will play two shows at The Great Hall at Lancaster University - historically a hotbed venue for the biggest names in music.

  • 99 reasons to head to Lancaster Music Co-op fundraiser

    A mix of surf, punk, psychobilly, 60s garage rock, spaghetti western soundtracks and outsider country and blues is in the offing this Friday as Lancaster Music Co-op hosts its latest monthly fundraiser at The Pub.

  • Popular Lancaster musician performs new single live on Beyond Radio

    Popular local singer-songwriter, Molly Warburton, paid a visit to the Beyond Radio studios to surprise one of our presenters.

Latest News from Radio News Hub

  • Home Office plans to spend 22bn of foreign aid on asylum support this year

    The Home Office plans to spend about Â£2.2 billion of foreign aid to support asylum seekers this financial year, according to new figures..

  • Its a Stormy Saturday

    Thunderstorm warning issued as parts of England and Wales face weekend deluge.

  • NHS set for boost of up to 30bn

    The NHS is expected to receive a funding boost of up to Â£30 billion in the spending review next week at the expense of other public services..

  • Hundreds apply to compensation scheme set up after armed forces LGBT ban

    More than 1,200 Scots have applied for payments from a compensation scheme set up to help members of the military impacted by a ban on LGBT personnel serving in the forces..

Advertising Features

  • ADVERTISING FEATURE: Landlords past and present celebrate 40 years of historic Lancaster pub

    A historic Lancaster pub located inside 17th century wine cellars is celebrating its 40th birthday.

  • Cosy Homes in Lancashire - your local council scheme

    Cosy Homes is offering Government funded grants for heating, insulation and more.

  • ADVERTISING FEATURE: Student Housing Lancaster celebrates 20-year milestone

    A business helping students to find quality accommodation in Lancaster is celebrating a significant milestone.

  • ADVERTISING FEATURE: Eden Private Hire goes from strength to strength

    Eden Private Hire is going from strength to strength providing taxi, private hire and coach hire services throughout the local area.
